properties.titulo_evento: Multibillion-dollar contract secures ‘major step forward’ for GCAP fighter jet
properties.descricao: Japan, Italy and the U.K. have handed a £4.6 billion ($6.1 billion) development contract to their national champions building the GCAP jet.

properties.titulo_evento: Poland teams up with Spain to double tanker aircraft purchase
properties.descricao: The agreement with Madrid will allow Warsaw to increase its tanker order to four units.

properties.titulo_evento: Taiwan needs a ‘hornet’s nest’ of drones to deter conflict, US diplomat says
properties.descricao: Taiwan says it needs to bolster its defenses in the face of a stepped-up threat from China, which claims the island as its own territory.

properties.titulo_evento: Kyiv military chief salutes outgoing US Army commander as war rages in Ukraine
properties.descricao: In a farewell message, Syrskyi credited the departing U.S. commander with saving thousands of Ukrainian lives.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia bombards Kyiv in one of war’s biggest strikes, at least 21 people killed
properties.descricao: Multiple explosions shook central Kyiv and reverberated across the capital throughout the night as thousands of residents rushed to bomb shelters.

properties.titulo_evento: UK ratchets up nuclear spending, with new warhead and delivery planes in the works
properties.descricao: Central to the current package is Astraea, the warhead designated A21/Mk7 that the Atomic Weapons Establishment has been developing since 2020.

properties.descricao: Kyiv's drones disrupt St Petersburg internet and flights as Russian strikes halt a gas facility in central Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Cape Verde fans overjoyed with team’s World Cup run despite Argentina loss
properties.descricao: Tiny island nation's colossal performance against reigning world champions leaves supporters feeling incredibly proud.

properties.descricao: A thrilling Golden Boot race hots up, France in scintillating form, and Messi and Argentina squeak past Cape Verde.

properties.descricao: After a genocidal war and famine, leading Israeli politicians say they are now ready to settle Gaza.

properties.titulo_evento: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
properties.descricao: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

properties.descricao: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

properties.descricao: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

properties.titulo_evento: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
properties.descricao: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

properties.titulo_evento: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats
properties.descricao: Sarah Rainsford reports from the completely destroyed nine-storey block of flats in Ukraine's capital.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump warns of communist threat as Washington parade cancelled
properties.descricao: "Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty," Trump said during a speech. "It is the greatest threat to our country".

properties.titulo_evento: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election in runoff
properties.descricao: Voters were primarily concerned with increasing levels of crime, especially extortion by violent organized crime gangs, and Fujimori pledged to combat crime with an iron fist.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran FM confirms reports Israel planned to target Tehran's negotiators
properties.descricao: Iran's foreign minister corroborated US media reports that Washington warned Tehran Israel may have had its chief negotiators on a target list during April talks in Islamabad, the same talks that produced the framework deal signed on 17 June.

properties.titulo_evento: Venezuela's interim leader defends earthquake response as death toll tops 2,500
properties.descricao: Delcy Rodríguez has hit back at criticism over her government’s response to the quakes, rejecting accusations of slow rescue efforts and claims that official casualty figures have been under-reported.

properties.titulo_evento: Dogs don Argentina shirts as World Cup fever grips Buenos Aires
properties.descricao: A dog walker in Buenos Aires leads dogs dressed in Argentina jerseys through the city as World Cup fever grows and fans celebrate in the streets.

properties.titulo_evento: Firefighters battle major wildfire in southern France
properties.descricao: A major wildfire in France's Aude department burns 900 hectares as aircraft and firefighters battle the blaze and evacuees reach emergency shelters.

properties.titulo_evento: 250 years of US independence: How France helped turn the tide of the Revolutionary War
properties.descricao: After months of discreet support, France officially allied itself with the American revolutionaries in their war against the British Empire, a decision that would permanently alter the fate of both nations.

properties.titulo_evento: Pope defends migrants on symbolic visit to key immigration hub Lampedusa
properties.descricao: Pope Leo XIV arrived on Saturday on Italy's Lampedusa island, a major port of call for migrants who risked the perilous crossing from North Africa, in a symbolic rebuke to the anti-immigrant rhetoric coming from US and EU political factions.

properties.titulo_evento: England-Mexico World Cup match could be moved earlier due to storm risk
properties.descricao: The round of 16 World Cup match between England and Mexico could be moved forward to noon local time on Sunday due to a risk of storms and flodding near Mexico City's Aztecaon stadium, local media reported. Fifa has yet to confirm the time change.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump set to put NATO unity to the test at Turkey summit
properties.descricao: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte is working to keep the alliance united and Donald Trump engaged. But disputes over burden sharing, US military commitments, and the Iran war are creating a major test for NATO unity.

properties.titulo_evento: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
properties.descricao: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran: Khamenei's funeral ceremonies officially begin
properties.descricao: Huge crowds have gathered in Tehran to pay their final respects to Iran's supreme leader, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran.

properties.descricao: The NATO alliance's European members and Canada have signaled a readiness to fully take over the financing of Ukraine's fight against Russia.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany: Merz rebukes Trump jab over defense at Baltic event
properties.descricao: In a meeting with Baltic leaders, Germany's Chancellor Friedrich Merz played up Germany's defense spending. It comes after new criticism from President Donald Trump that Germany is lagging behind other NATO allies.

properties.titulo_evento: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
properties.descricao: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.
